title: Dark Health Data: an open pipeline turning buried public-health documents into research-ready datasets
description:
An open-source pipeline that converts buried public-record health and healthcare documents (PDFs) into open, research-ready datasets via large language model extraction with a verification layer: source grounding, symbolic and Logical Neural Network-inspired neurosymbolic constraint checks, a heterogeneous model ensemble, and a conformal selective-acceptance gate. Every record carries full provenance and a quality/trust score; problems are flagged, never imputed or dropped.

Connectors: Medicaid External Quality Review (EQR) technical reports, hospital Community Health Needs Assessments (CHNA), Maternal Mortality Review Committee (MMRC) reports, Medicaid Section 1115 demonstration evaluations, and nursing-home CMS-2567 statements of deficiency. This release adds a regression-safe incremental merge workflow (expansion only ever adds coverage), a Message Batches extraction path, and a TLS-fallback fetch for public servers with incomplete certificate chains. The extracted datasets are released separately under CC0 (see Related identifiers).

creators.name: Basu, Sanjay
creators.affiliation: University of California, San Francisco; Waymark
